purpose: Hand-checked candidate bridges from the hand-authored kernel-v0 (54 Explicated profile-1 concepts) and molecules-v0 (54 molecules) into the onto-obo bulk tier (BFO/RO/GO). These are ANNOTATION-layer bridge candidates (categorial correspondences), NOT identity claims. BFO/RO are a foundational realist ontology and gUFO (which kernel-v0 already targets: gufo:Event, gufo:Kind) is a BFO-adjacent lightweight UFO; kernel-v0 concepts are NSM explications. The correspondence is 'this NSM concept falls under this BFO category / uses this RO relation', not sameConceptAs. Nothing here is endorsed and nothing enters the mapper.
method: manual review by the extracting agent (2026-07-07) against the pinned onto-obo records. Only correspondences the agent is confident of are listed; confidence + caveat per link. gUFO is the intended bridge target for the Event/Kind alignments (kernel-v0 uses gufo: URIs); BFO is cited because gUFO's categories are BFO-aligned and BFO is what we extracted.
confidenceScale: high = the NSM concept clearly instantiates the BFO category / RO relation; medium = a defensible categorial fit with a known gap; low = suggestive only.

alignments.note: kernel-v0:event (gufo:Event: 'this something happens at some time; after this time it is not happening') is a BFO occurrent, specifically a process. The directive's named anchor (BFO continuant/occurrent <-> our Event). Also fallsUnder BFO:0000003 (occurrent).
The genus one level up from process; gufo:Event corresponds to BFO occurrent (something with temporal parts that unfolds in time).

kernel-v0:part_of ('X is a part of Y') is exactly RO/BFO parthood BFO:0000050. Caveat: RO parthood carries temporal-index + category-compatibility semantics (continuant/occurrent) that the NSM explication leaves implicit.
kernel-v0:has_part ('Y is a part of X; X is one something') is the inverse relation BFO:0000051, the has-part converse of BFO:0000050.

kernel-v0:cause ('X happens; because X happens, Y happens after') is a directed causal-influence-between-occurrents relation, narrower than RO's symmetric-closure 'causally related to'; the closest directed RO term is RO:0002411 'causally upstream of'. RO's causal relations are biological-process-scoped.

kernel-v0:kind (gufo:Kind: a rigid sortal, 'it cannot not be of this kind') is a META-type of continuants (a kind classifies endurants), not a BFO class itself; BFO has no rigidity meta-level. Low-confidence categorial hint only.

Suggestive only: kernel-v0:alive ('this someone lives now; the body can move') sits over the biological-process domain GO axiomatizes, but GO defines processes of organisms, not the predicate 'is alive'. Recorded to mark the domain touch-point, not a real bridge.

The inanimate artefact/natural-object molecules are BFO objects (BFO:0000030, a material entity that is spatially/causally unified and maximally self-connected). Medium: the molecule set is not individuated by BFO's unity criteria.

Living-thing molecules are BFO objects (material entities). A tighter bridge would be to a species/organism class in NCBITaxon or an anatomical-object class; recorded at BFO object level to stay within the extracted set.

Body-part molecules are BFO fiat object parts (BFO:0000024) or objects, part_of an organism. The eye/hand demarcation is anatomical; UBERON is the true home. part_of (BFO:0000050) relates them to the organism molecule.

The bodily-activity molecules denote BFO processes (occurrents), with the animate molecule as RO participant (RO:0000056 'participates in'). Medium: NSM verb molecules also carry manner/telic content BFO process leaves unspecified.

notAligned.note: Deliberately unaligned: the emotion/epistemic kernel concepts (afraid, angry, happy, sad, believe, know, forget, remember) — BFO's specifically-dependent-continuant qualities/dispositions (BFO:0000019/0000016) are a categorial home but the NSM mental-predicate content is not what BFO axiomatizes; the artefact-history concepts (bookmark, archived, reminder, memory) — these are information/social-artefact concepts (IAO/gUFO territory, not core BFO); and the speech-act concepts (promise, lie, condolence) — social acts, out of BFO scope.
